steps.title: Recursion tree.
steps.body:
Level $i$: $3^i$ subproblems of size $n/4^i$. Non-recursive work per subproblem $= n/4^i$. Level work $= 3^i \cdot n/4^i = n \cdot (3/4)^i$.

Decreasing geometric with ratio $3/4$.

$$\sum_{i=0}^{\log_4 n - 1} n(3/4)^i \le n \sum_{i=0}^{\infty} (3/4)^i = \frac{n}{1 - 3/4} = 4n.$$

Leaves: $3^{\log_4 n} = n^{\log_4 3} \approx n^{0.79}$, cost $1$ each → $o(n)$ overall.

**Total: $T(n) = \Theta(n)$.**

steps.title: Inductive proof.
steps.body:
**Claim:** $T(n) \le Cn$ for $C = 16/13$ (or any $C \ge 4$). Actually $C = 4$ is simplest.

**Base $n=1$:** $T(1) = 1 \le 4$. ✓

**I.H.:** $T(k) \le 4k$ for all $k < n$.

**Step:** $T(n) = 3T(n/4) + n \le 3 \cdot 4 \cdot (n/4) + n = 3n + n = 4n$. ✓

So $T(n) = O(n)$; combined with $T(n) \ge n$: $T(n) = \Theta(n)$.

tips.label: Choosing $C$ via the recurrence rule
tips.content: For $T(n) = aT(n/b) + n$ with $a/b < 1$: pick $C \ge 1/(1 - a/b)$. Here $a/b = 3/4$, so $C \ge 4$.
